title: catha glauca
intro: Catha glauca, also known as khat or Arabian tea, is a shrub native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, belonging to the Celastraceae family of plants. The leaves of Catha glauca contain the alkaloids cathinone and cathine, which are responsible for its stimulant and psychoactive effects. Medicinally, Catha glauca has been used to treat conditions such as fatigue, depression, and appetite loss, and it has also been used to enhance mental alertness and cognitive function. The leaves can be consumed as a tea, chewed fresh, or made into a powder for use in herbal preparations, although its use is regulated in many countries due to concerns over addiction and other potential health risks.
